_meta.coverage_note: FULL CORPUS — all 31 August dates. August is the most feast-dense month of the Byzantine year. It contains: the Procession of the Cross (Aug 1, rank 3), the Transfiguration (Aug 6, rank 1 Great Feast), the Dormition of the Theotokos (Aug 15, rank 1 Great Feast), and the Translation of the Image of Christ Not Made by Hands (Aug 16, rank 3). The Dormition Fast runs Aug 1–14. Dates with unconfirmed troparion text carry troparion_status:'text-unavailable'.

commemorations.name: Procession of the Precious Cross; Holy Maccabee Martyrs

troparion.title: Troparion of the Procession of the Cross
troparion.text: Save, O Lord, Thy people and bless Thine inheritance; grant victories to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries; and by virtue of Thy Cross, preserve Thy habitation.

commemorations.notes: Procession of the Precious and Life-giving Cross + commemoration of the Seven Maccabee Martyrs and their mother Solomonia. Rank 3. Beginning of the Dormition Fast. The Cross troparion is the standard troparion of the Exaltation of the Cross.

commemorations.name: Translation of the Relics of the Protomartyr Stephen

troparion.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Stephen (Translation)
troparion.text: Thy crown of martyrdom overflows with glory, O first-born of the martyrs; for thou didst suffer with Christ the King of all, and receive from His hand the crown of victory. Intercede with Christ our God that He may save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Translation of relics of the Protomartyr Stephen (415 AD). Uses Stephen's standard troparion. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Venerable Isaakios, Dalmatos, and Faustus of the Dalmatian Monastery

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Isaakios, Dalmatos, and Faustus
troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, always act with kindness towards us; take not Your mercy from us, but guide our lives in peace through the prayers of Venerable Isaac, Dalmatus, and Faustus.

commemorations.notes: 4th–5th-c. fathers of the Dalmatian Monastery in Constantinople: Isaakios (+383, also May 30 and March 22), Dalmatos (archimandrite, +after 446), Faustus (Dalmatos's son, igumen after him). Tone 4 joint troparion confirmed OCA August 3 all-troparia page (direct fetch). Queue held Tone 8 — corrected to Tone 4.

commemorations.name: The Seven Youths (Sleepers) of Ephesus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Seven Youths (Sleepers) of Ephesus
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, the Seven Sleepers of Ephesus, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: The Seven Sleepers of Ephesus (+3rd c., awakened 5th c.). Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Transfiguration of Our Lord

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of the Transfiguration
troparion.text: Come, let us go up to the mount of the Lord and to the house of our God, and let us behold the glory of His Transfiguration, the glory as of the Only-begotten of the Father. Let us receive light from His light, with singing let us magnify Christ forever.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Transfiguration. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: The Holy Transfiguration of Our Lord God and Savior Jesus Christ

troparion.title: Troparion of the Transfiguration
troparion.text: Thou wast transfigured on the mount, O Christ our God, revealing Thy glory to Thy disciples as far as they could bear it. Let Thine everlasting Light shine upon us sinners, through the intercessions of the Theotokos. O Giver of Light, glory to Thee.

commemorations.notes: Great Feast — Transfiguration of Our Lord. Rank 1. One of the Twelve Great Feasts.

commemorations.name: Venerable Martyr Dometios of Persia

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Martyr Dometios of Persia
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Dometios, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Dometios of Persia (+363), monk and martyr under Julian the Apostate. Type is 'martyr' in batch; promoted accordingly. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: St. Emilian the Confessor, Bishop of Cyzicus

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Emilian the Confessor, Bishop of Cyzicus
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarch Father Emilian, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Emilian of Cyzicus (+9th c.), confessor bishop under the Second Iconoclasm. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Matthias

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Matthias
troparion.text: O Holy Apostle Matthias, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Matthias the Apostle, chosen to replace Judas (Acts 1:26). Rank 3. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Lawrence, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Lawrence of Rome (+258), archdeacon and martyr under Valerian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique Eastern text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Euplus (Euplius) of Catania

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Euplus of Catania
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Euplus, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Euplus of Catania (+304), deacon-martyr under Diocletian. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Venerable Maximus the Confessor

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Maximus the Confessor
troparion.text: Guide of Orthodoxy, pillar and teacher of piety, luminary of the world and adornment of monks: O God-bearing Maximus, thou hast enlightened all by thy teachings. O harp of the Spirit, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Maximus the Confessor (+662), theologian and defender of Chalcedonian Christology against Monothelitism. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Dormition of the Most Holy Theotokos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of the Dormition
troparion.text: In thy birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy falling asleep thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated to life, O Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions dost deliver our souls from death.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Dormition. The Dormition Troparion is used. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: The Dormition of Our Most Holy Lady, the Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary

troparion.title: Troparion of the Dormition of the Theotokos
troparion.text: In thy birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy falling asleep thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated to life, O Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions dost deliver our souls from death.

commemorations.notes: Great Feast — Dormition (Falling Asleep) of the Theotokos. Rank 1. One of the Twelve Great Feasts. Leavetaking Aug 23.

commemorations.name: Translation of the Image of Our Lord Jesus Christ Not Made by Hands (Holy Mandylion)

troparion.title: Troparion of the Image Not Made by Hands
troparion.text: We venerate Thine immaculate image, O Good One, and ask forgiveness of our transgressions, O Christ our God; for by Thy will Thou wast pleased to ascend the Cross in Thy flesh to deliver from the bondage of the enemy those whom Thou hadst fashioned. Wherefore, we thankfully cry aloud to Thee: Thou didst fill all with joy, O our Savior, Thou Who camest to save the world.

commemorations.notes: Translation of the Holy Mandylion (Image Not Made by Hands) from Edessa to Constantinople (944 AD). Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Myron of Cyzicus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Myron of Cyzicus
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Myron, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Myron of Cyzicus (+3rd c.), presbyter-martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Floros and Lavros

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyrs Floros and Lavros
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Floros and Lavros, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Floros and Lavros (+2nd c.), stonemason-martyrs of Illyria. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Andrew Stratelates and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Andrew Stratelates and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Andrew Stratelates and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Andrew Stratelates and 2,593 companions (+4th c.), martyrs of Cilicia. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Samuel

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Prophet Samuel
troparion.text: We celebrate the memory of Thy prophet Samuel, O Lord, and through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Samuel the Prophet (11th c. BC). Rank 4. Standard prophet troparion form.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Thaddaeus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Thaddaeus
troparion.text: O Holy Apostle Thaddaeus, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Thaddaeus, one of the Seventy Apostles (distinct from Jude-Thaddaeus), evangelist of Edessa. Rank 4. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Agathonikos and companions

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Martyr Agathonikos and companions
troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Agathonikos and companions, O Lord, through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through their intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Agathonikos and companions (+3rd c.), martyrs of Nicomedia. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4, plural form applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Leavetaking of the Dormition of the Theotokos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Dormition (Leavetaking)
troparion.text: In thy birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy falling asleep thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated to life, O Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions dost deliver our souls from death.

commemorations.notes: Leavetaking of the Dormition. Dormition Troparion repeated. Rank 4.

commemorations.name: Hieromartyr Eutyches, Disciple of John the Theologian

troparion.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Eutyches, Disciple of John the Theologian
troparion.text: Your holy martyr Eutyches, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Eutyches (+1st c.), disciple of John and Paul, bishop and martyr. Rank 4. Standard martyr troparion Tone 4 applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Titus

troparion.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Titus
troparion.text: O Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Titus, intercede with the merciful God, that He grant our souls forgiveness of offences.

commemorations.notes: Bartholomew (one of the Twelve) and Titus (companion of Paul). Joint feast Aug 25. Rank 3. Standard apostle troparion form (Tone 3).

commemorations.name: St. Tikhon of Zadonsk, Bishop of Voronezh

troparion.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon of Zadonsk
troparion.text: To the heritage of Christ thou wast a shepherd and teacher; thou didst accomplish the command of the Apostle Paul: 'I have become all things to all men.' O hierarch Tikhon, beseech Christ God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Tikhon of Zadonsk (+1783), Bishop of Voronezh, Russian monastic theologian. Glorified 1861. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Venerable Pimen (Poemen) the Great

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Pimen the Great
troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! Our Father Pimen, pray to Christ God to save our souls!

commemorations.notes: Egyptian desert abba (+450), one of the principal Apophthegmata fathers. Tone 8 confirmed OCA August 27 all-troparia page, listed as the primary troparion for Pimen the Great. Standard 'flood of tears' Tone 8 monastic troparion. Live tone was already 8 — tone was correct, text was missing.

commemorations.name: Venerable Moses the Ethiopian (the Black)

troparion.title: Troparion of the Venerable Moses the Ethiopian
troparion.text: You abandoned the Egypt of the passions, O Father, ascending the mount of the virtues with fervent faith, taking the Cross of Christ upon your shoulders; and being glorified in godly works, you proved to be a model for monastics, O Moses, summit of the Fathers. With them, pray unceasingly that our souls may find mercy.

commemorations.notes: Egyptian desert father of Ethiopian origin (+405), martyred at Scete. Tone 1 primary troparion confirmed OCA August 28 all-troparia page (listed first). OCA also lists a secondary Tone 1 Podoben text ('When the stone had been sealed'); the non-Podoben primary text is used here. Live entry had troparion_tone: 8 — corrected to Tone 1.

commemorations.name: The Beheading of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner, and Baptist John

troparion.title: Troparion of the Beheading of John the Baptist
troparion.text: The memory of the righteous is celebrated with praise, but the Lord's testimony is sufficient for thee, O Forerunner; for thou hast proved to be truly more venerable than the Prophets, since thou wast granted to baptize in the stream Him Whom they proclaimed. Wherefore, having contested for the truth, thou didst rejoice to announce to those in Hades the good tidings of God Who had appeared in the flesh; Who takes away the sin of the world, and grants us great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Beheading of John the Baptist. Rank 2 (Polyeleos). A strict fast day. Same Troparion tone as the Synaxis of the Baptist (Jan 7).

commemorations.name: Sts. Alexander, John, and Paul the New, Patriarchs of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of Sts. Alexander, John, and Paul the New, Patriarchs of Constantinople
troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flocks as rules of faith, images of humility and teachers of abstinence. Your humility exalted you; your poverty enriched you. Hierarchs Alexander, John, and Paul, entreat Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Alexander (+340), John (+595), Paul the New (+784), patriarchs of Constantinople. Rank 4. Standard hierarch troparion Tone 4, plural adaptation applied. No unique text confirmed at OCA; standard form used per corpus rules.

commemorations.name: Deposition of the Precious Belt of the Most Holy Theotokos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Deposition of the Belt of the Theotokos
troparion.text: O Theotokos, thou art a vine grown from a noble root; thou hast blossomed as a blossom of incorruption; thou dost grant healing to all. We thy servants bless thee with faith and call thee blessed, as thou hast commanded.

commemorations.notes: Deposition of the Belt of the Theotokos at Constantinople (395 AD). Rank 3. Distinct from the Deposition of the Robe (Jul 2).
